patch.value: Public SDK transaction boundary: atomic multi-item + relationship-event commit primitive

The pm-vcs exemplar (PR#587) exposed the one waived SDK gap in packages/pm-vcs/GAP_REPORT.md: there is no public transaction boundary committing a multi-item mutation together with relationship events. The exemplar compensates with a bespoke crash-consistency protocol (event-first write order, deterministic merge event identity, idempotent duplicate-append recovery, fail-closed conflicts) that every foreign-domain package would otherwise have to reinvent. Ship a first-class SDK commit primitive: all-or-nothing observable state across item store + relationship event stream, crash-safe at every partial-write point, preserving immutable-history append-only semantics (no rollback rewrites — compensation/reconciliation on replay). Migrate pm-vcs merge onto it as the acceptance vehicle, or record precisely why the bespoke protocol must remain.

Public SDK primitive commits multi-item mutations + relationship events with all-or-nothing observable state; failure-injection coverage for every partial-write direction and crash point; pm-vcs merge migrated onto the primitive (or a recorded justified waiver); contract docs + SDK.md published; no history-stream rewrite semantics introduced

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A transaction journal is a recovery hint, never stronger than current domain inspection: validate lifecycle invariants at load, rebuild completion from inspections, and make every compensation conditional on still owning the exact state it intends to reverse.

patch.value: PR #605 review-hardened public transaction primitive complete: crash recovery validates journals and current state, compensation is transaction-owned, VCS acceptance is atomic, all hosted findings are handled, linked tests and exact 100% coverage pass, and packed SDK/VCS proof succeeds.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Pre-push Greptile review 83e0a668-645f-4229-89fe-889358d7a38e found one valid P1: compensation discovery could throw before persisting compensating state, leaving an applying journal without an automatic reconciliation path. Reopened only this canonical transaction item for the focused recovery-state correction.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Pre-push Greptile 83e0a668 P1 resolved: compensation now writes durable compensating state before any domain inspection, reconciles all steps in reverse order, and invokes each step's idempotent transaction-owned compensation when inspection itself fails. A regression reproduces an inspection conflict after an earlier step applied, verifies the journal reaches compensated without manual deletion, clears the conflict, and proves the next attempt commits. Verification: focused 14 tests pass, strict static/docstring/package/token gates pass, and full repository coverage passes 311 files / 5,917 tests at exact 100/100/100/100 (39,077 statements, 27,480 branches, 7,957 functions, 38,043 lines).

author: codex-root
message: Hosted round 3: persist per-step compensation ownership across process crashes and make discovery updates atomic

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted round 3 accepted findings: Greptile identified that process-local VCS ownership is lost after apply-before-journal-record crashes; Gemini identified partial in-memory journal result mutation if a later inspection throws and an unsafe ENOENT property cast. Scope is a public durable per-step provenance contract plus atomic discovery and safe error narrowing, with VCS crash acceptance.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Hosted round 3 repairs complete: the public transaction journal now records startedStepIds before invoking apply and atomically stores prepareCompensation JSON data required to restore pre-mutation state after process restart. Compensation is restricted to transaction-started steps and receives durable data; VCS no longer uses process-local ownership. discoverAppliedSteps swaps a temporary result map only after every inspection succeeds, and ENOENT narrowing is safe for unknown throws. New acceptance covers apply-before-record crash + later ledger conflict + original-ref restoration, pre-existing merge preservation, malformed compensation data, atomic discovery failure/retry, and inspection failure during owned compensation. Verification: focused 15 tests; 311 files / 5,918 tests exact 100/100/100/100 (39,105 statements, 27,502 branches, 7,967 functions, 38,072 lines); typecheck, ESLint, zero clones, complexity, 100% docstrings, SDK boundary, token budget pass. Packed proof /tmp/pm-sdk-durable-compensation-proof-hsDup4 installed the tarball and local pm-vcs, completed ref/create/propose/merge/log, restored packed compensation pre-state, and passed isolated deep package doctor with zero warnings/blockers.
